untranslatable_reason: The bump-and-run reversal top formation is a visual price pattern requiring multi-bar morphology analysis (identification of the bump, run phase, and reversal point) that cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The breakout direction (DOWN) and volume trend (Upward) can be partially captured, but the core pattern identification—which relies on specific candlestick arrangement and trend structure—exceeds the scope of indicator-based rules. Stop placement rule is marked AUTHOR_SILENT in source material.
translation_notes: Entry condition uses upward volume as a proxy for the pattern's volume trend requirement. Exit uses a simple trend reversal crossover as the author provided no explicit stop or exit guidance. The 19%-27% average decline and 62%-65% pullback statistics from the source could inform position sizing or target placement externally, but cannot be encoded as entry/exit rules. A full implementation would require a dedicated pattern-recognition module or manual visual confirmation.
